pairs.question: Who developed Astellia Online, and when was it originally released?
pairs.answer: Astellia Online (sometimes just called Astellia) was developed by the Korean studio Studio 8. It was originally released in South Korea in late 2018.

pairs.question: How did the Western release of Astellia differ in terms of monetization compared to the Korean release?
pairs.answer: The Western release of Astellia was a buy-to-play model, meaning players had to purchase the game to access it, whereas the Korean release was free-to-play.

pairs.question: What are Astels in Astellia Online, and how do players use them?
pairs.answer: Astels are summonable companions in Astellia Online that players can use to complement their weaknesses or enhance their power. They are collectible within the game.

pairs.question: What was the fate of Astellia Online's service in the West, and when did it end?
pairs.answer: The game service for both Western versions of Astellia Online was terminated in October 2021.

pairs.question: What is Astellia Royal, and how does it differ from the original Astellia Online?
pairs.answer: Astellia Royal is a free-to-play version of Astellia released on Steam in March 2021. Unlike the original, it is free to access and play.

pairs.question: How did the NFT version of Astellia (Astel of Atra) utilize blockchain technology?
pairs.answer: In Astel of Atra, Astels are tradeable NFTs stored on the Polygon blockchain. Players can earn cryptocurrency in-game, but they must own an Astel NFT to do so.

pairs.question: Write a Python code snippet to check if a player's Astel NFT balance is sufficient to earn cryptocurrency in Astel of Atra.
Why was Astel of Atra considered one of the first play-to-earn blockchain MMORPGs?
pairs.answer: Astel of Atra was considered one of the first play-to-earn blockchain MMORPGs because it allowed players to earn cryptocurrency in-game by owning and trading Astel NFTs, a model that was innovative at the time and shared with other titles like MIR4.

pairs.question: What happened to the Korean website of Astellia by May 2023?
pairs.answer: By May 2023, the Korean website for Astellia appears to have been taken down or removed, indicating the end of official support or availability in that region.
